item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As fellow Ancient Egyptian god, SCP-208 seems like a shoe-in. Aside from that, there's also SCP-2514, which is a fellow ungulate housed in Area 12 that emits pyrotechnics, but I'm not sure how to work that in also its written by me but i hope thats okay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooh! I am the author of 3223, and I love Ancient Egyptian skips. This looks great to me.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

So, its strongly implied that the cow in this skip is, in fact, an Ancient Egyptian god. Being able to understand the mumbling of D-class that undergo SCP-2280's process would likely shine a light on this situation. The problem here, of course, arises from the fact that few people know what they are saying, with the closest language being Coptic Egyptian. I bet that one SCP, who is literally an Ancient Egyptian Priest, might be of help. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Instances of SCP-2280-1 resulting from D-class personnel have not responded to communication attempts, but have been heard mumbling in an unknown language. Linguistic analysis has not succeeded in translating their speech, but have noted similarities to languages in the Afroasiatic family, particularly Coptic Egyptian.

To this:

Instances of SCP-2280-1 resulting from D-class personnel have not responded to communication attempts, but have been heard mumbling in an unknown language. Linguistic analysis has not succeeded in translating their speech, but have noted similarities to languages in the Afroasiatic family, particularly Coptic Egyptian1.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

Footnotes
1. Use of SCP-3223-1 as a potential translator is currently pending review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Word disassociation!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After the fact. It was just too deliciously apropos to not use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … did you base the SCP on the Cicirega lyric, or did you realize that was a possibility for the title after the fact?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the setup, love the absorbing cow giving birth to light creatures.
This whole thing - this whole premise screams "J HAYES WOULD LOVE THIS"
But you managed to execute it in a way that just… leaves me like wtf. and not the good kind.
this needs more for an upvote.
+-0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy cow!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. I feel like it's some kind of analogy for how stars are created from the black void of space, or at least that's what I took from it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. I think it needs more detail to grab me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: High praise from you, Sorts! Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All of my yes

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe it is based on "Holy Diver".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not familiar with Dio's discography; which song is this based on?

Also: nicely done. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Sorts bowed his head because he knew that he'd been beat.
And he laid that golden fiddle on the ground at NovaeDeArx's feet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy Bovine
You stopped me cold won't set me free
What's becoming of me

Holy Bovine
You've crushed my bones and eaten me
Now you're gestating me

Time for my birthday
Holy Bovine

Shine like sunlight
Drink my fill then it's time to slip
The surly bonds of earth

-With apologies to Dio and common decency

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Obscure egyptian mythology+ bizzare reinterpretation= +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
black hole cow
won't you now
come milk out all the rain

black hole cow
won't you now

won't you now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I would have liked this a bit more if I knew more about Egyptian mythology up front. At first I thought you where going for a cow that eats vegetables. Overall, it's okay, but not great. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ I would guess that this is...
– hide block

…the goddess, Nut, who is well known to swallow the sun, moon, and stars at night, and give birth to them again at sunrise. She is often depicted as a black cow with stars on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Egyptian deity, yes; Hathor, no. The rest of the anomalous stuff is related to the other myths about this particular deity.

And yeah, I'm trying to shoop a decent pic, but my skills, they are lacking.

Glad you enjoyed it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Although the last bit about the cow just going 'SEE YA LATER, FUCKERS!!' and bouncing into the sky killed a lot of my enthusiasm, I remembered that this is a freaking black hole cow. Which is awesome. It's also based on the goddess Hathor, yeah? Which is awesome!

Upvoted, but two suggestions. First, have something substantial come of the last paragraph (or remove it), because right now it just seems like a tacked-on "Oh yeah, language we don't understand." Second, add a picture!! This is one of those things that would be infinitely cooler with a picture. (Even if it's just Photoshop's Burn tool rubbed over a cow. Edit: I just tried that and it looks really cool!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Considering the 'mom' is -constantly- absorbing all visible light, there's a lot of energy there, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's some nice stuff here; however, it doesn't feel like it was strung together into a cohesive whole. The short form SCP can work for simple ideas, but this feels like it could have benefited from some fleshing out. +-0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Black hole cow births sun creatures? Definitely sounds like mythology, even if I don't know what from. Still… what?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was into it. My only issue was that it seemed like an object putting off 100 kilolux needs to have a fuckton of energy in it, more than comes out of eating an…i dunno, elk or something. I EXPECT NAUGHT BUT THE PUREST SCIENCE FROM YOU, DREWBEAR

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it…but I feel like it stops rather abruptly. You've left me curious enough to know what is going on, but it doesn't feel like it was finished to me. Where do the calves go? What did autopsy show on the stillborns? Have we recovered a live 2280-1 instance? What happens to instances we prevent from suckling at the mother? A bunch of other questions that I feel like the Foundation would have attempted to answer just weren't answered.

E: upvoted

I don't want to downvote, because I really like the concept and the promise…but I don't know if I'm going to upvote either. Neutral vote for now, and I'll read it again later on today and decide then how to cast my vote. Hopefully.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since most people don't use lux very often, I would add a footnote saying that 100,000 lux is approximately the maximum brightness of direct sunlight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would be cool, but it wouldn't change the content of the article, so I would still leave this at a downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm thinking about adding a pic of the cow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope. It is a cow from myth, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Didn't really interest me. I like how the bull thing looks, but that's about the only thing I liked.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it the Apis Bull?

EDIT: Oh. Bull. Cow. Male. Female. Whoops.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mooooo
